reviewed_map.rationale: Cresta's platform is built to turn every customer conversation into a competitive advantage, providing voice agent assistance for customer support, sales, and operational workflows. This focus on enterprise customer interaction places it squarely in the customer service and sales vertical.

Fixie develops speech‑native voice AI agents and APIs that enable real‑time, human‑like conversations with low latency and preserved paralinguistic cues. Its target audience is developers building conversational voice experiences, fitting the voice AI developer platform vertical.

We're the secret behind some of the world's best performing voice agents:
""|
— 11x, Head of Growth
The Voice AI Problem
Human Speech ≠ Text
Most Voice AI systems are designed to convert speech to text before an LLM can process it. This approach introduces two problems:
1) Transcribing speech to text adds latency, slowing the conversation before inference can even begin.
2) Paralinguistic signals that influence meaning, like tone, cadence, and pitch, are lost when speech is transcribed.
This is why most Voice AI agents feel slow and robotic. Achievin

retrieved_docs.title: HuggingFace Blog — Reachymini Appstore
retrieved_docs.snippet:
HuggingFace Blog — Reachymini Appstore

Introducing the agentic robotics appstore for 10,000 Reachy Minis
Today we're shipping an agentic toolkit for Reachy Mini, our open-source desktop robot. You describe the behavior you want in plain English, and an AI agent writes, tests, ships the code to the robot and iterates with you to get it working. No SDK to learn, no robotics background needed.
If you have a Reachy Mini (or just want to play in the browser simulator), you can get started right now by asking your favorite agent something like:
Help me build a Reachy Mini app that waves and says hello when
someone walks into the room.
Use the open-source code at https://github.com/pollen-robotics/reachy_mini
and the docs at https://huggingface.co/docs/reachy_mini/index
So far, the community has shipped over 200 apps, built by 150+ different creators, most of whom had never written robotics code before. Last week another batch of almost 3,000 robots went out to customers worldwide, taking the install base to nearly 10,000 units in the wild. More than 1,000 additional units will ship in the next 30 days.
For sixty years, robots were built by roboticists. When the hardware is open-source, the software is open-source, and an AI a

retrieved_docs.title: TechCrunch: Agentic AI Funding Surge in Early 2026
retrieved_docs.snippet:
TechCrunch: Agentic AI Funding Surge in Early 2026

TechCrunch reports that agentic AI startups raised over $4B in Q1 2026, with the majority of deals concentrated in orchestration infrastructure, enterprise RAG platforms, and agent evaluation tooling. Notable rounds include CrewAI's $100M Series B, a $60M raise by Composio, and continued growth at Langfuse. Investors cite the shift from single-turn chatbots to persistent, multi-step agents as the key driver of demand.

Cresta — about

Cresta by the numbers

Turning every conversation into a competitive advantage
Exceptional customer experiences are the foundation of every great business. That's why we're on a mission to unlock the full potential of every customer conversation. How? With human-centric agentic AI that delights customers with intelligent automation, empowers humans to work smarter and faster, and turns strategic insights into better business outcomes.
View careers
Our story
2017
- Cresta is born at Stanford’s AI Lab, co-founded by Sebastian Thrun
- He's joined by co-founders Tim Shi, an early researcher at OpenAI, and Zayd Enam
2018
- Cresta deploys a transformer-based model for real-time Agent Assist at Intuit, its first customer
2020
- Cresta emerges from stealth with Series A backing from Greylock and a16z
2021
- Cresta raises its Series B from Sequoia as revenue quadruples
- Expands beyond digital with Voice Agent Assist
2022
- Cresta raises Series C led by Tiger Global
- Secures multiple conversational AI patents
2023
- Ping Wu (Co-founder of Google CCAI) becomes CEO
- Launches Opera, a no-code AI orchestration engine, and conversation intelligence
2

retrieved_docs.title: YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure
retrieved_docs.snippet:
YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure

Y Combinator's Winter 2025 Request for Startups highlights AI infrastructure as a top investment thesis. Key areas include: developer tools for agentic AI (debugging, testing, deployment), evaluation frameworks for LLM reliability, multi-modal data pipelines, and cost-optimization layers for large language model inference. YC notes that startups solving agent observability and grounding are especially well-positioned.

retrieved_docs.title: Qdrant Blog — case-study-deutsche-telekom
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Qdrant Blog — case-study-deutsche-telekom

How Deutsche Telekom Built a Multi-Agent Enterprise Platform Leveraging Qdrant
Manuel Meyer
·March 07, 2025
On this page:
How Deutsche Telekom Built a Scalable, Multi-Agent Enterprise Platform Leveraging Qdrant—Powering Over 2 Million Conversations Across Europe
Arun Joseph, who leads engineering and architecture for Deutsche Telekom’s AI Competence Center (AICC), faced a critical challenge: how do you efficiently and scalably deploy AI-powered assistants across a vast enterprise ecosystem? The goal was to deploy GenAI for customer sales and service operations to resolve customer queries faster across the 10 countries where Deutsche Telekom operates in Europe.
To achieve this, Telekom developed Frag Magenta OneBOT (Eng: Ask Magenta), a platform that includes chatbots and voice bots, built as a Platform as a Service (PaaS) to ensure scalability across Deutsche Telekom’s ten European subsidiaries.
“We knew from the start that we couldn’t just deploy RAG, tool calling, and workflows at scale without a platform-first approach,” Arun explains. “When I looked at the challenge, it looked a lot like a distributed systems and engineering challenge, not just an AI problem.”
Key Requirements

retrieved_docs.title: Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025

The Latent Space newsletter surveys the agentic AI platform landscape as of mid-2025. The analysis identifies three dominant approaches: (1) orchestration frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph) focused on multi-agent coordination; (2) RAG-first platforms (LlamaIndex, Vertex AI Search) focused on grounding agents in enterprise data; and (3) evaluation-native platforms (Langfuse, Braintrust, PromptLayer) focused on reliability and measurement. The newsletter argues that evaluation will become the key differentiator as models commoditize.

retrieved_docs.title: Tavily — about
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Tavily — about

/about us
The agentic web is vast and unstructured. Tavily delivers high-quality, fresh, and structured web context, making it easy for agents to stay grounded as they scale from prototypes to production.
/our mission
Our mission is to help AI agents navigate the web quickly and efficiently by shaping the context they see. We prioritize relevance, freshness, and efficiency so agents reason accurately without wasting tokens.
/team
We’re a team of collaborators, builders, and deep thinkers, obsessed with making reliable web access possible for AI agents in the real world. We move quickly, support each other, and stay focused on shipping systems that deliver real impact in production.

retrieved_docs.title: LangGraph: Stateful Agent Orchestration with Graphs
retrieved_docs.snippet:
LangGraph: Stateful Agent Orchestration with Graphs

LangGraph, built on top of LangChain, provides a graph-based abstraction for building reliable, stateful LLM agents. Developers define nodes (agent steps) and edges (transitions) in a directed graph, enabling cyclic workflows, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and fine-grained state management. LangGraph is widely used for production-grade agentic pipelines requiring persistence and controllability.
